author: Kevin Thomas
text: By 'IDE freeze', I mean that the window containing the project stops repainting forever, but this is recoverable by closing the window and reopening it. This applies to popups as well as project windows. While the window itself stops painting, some things like tooltips and existing popup menus can behave differently.

author: Kevin Thomas
text: However, it changes the selection to the working copy, even if abandoning a different change. It would be better to select one of the abandoned change's parents (or even all of them).

author: Kevin Thomas
text:
Using the following prompt:

Create me a logo for an IntelliJ IDEA plugin called jj-idea, that integrates the Jujutsu version control system into IDEA. I would like a simple, minimalist log, ideally inspired by elements of both the IntelliJ IDEA logo (e.g. https://icon-icons.com/icon/intellij-macos-bigsur/190061) and the Jujutsu logo (e.g. https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/images/jj-logo.svg).

It needs to work with the following dimensions and aspect ratio:
  • Canvas: 40×40px
  • Visible area: 36×36px (2px transparent padding on all sides)
  • Shape guidelines:
    • Square: 32×32px
    • Circle: 36px diameter
    • Horizontal rectangle: 36×26px
    • Vertical rectangle: 26×36px

Ideally <3Kb SVG

  • Ensure visibility on both light and dark backgrounds, or provide both light and dark mode variants
  • Match visual weight of existing marketplace logos
  • Use technology's colors when available

Incorporate coral #FF6B6B

author: Kevin Thomas
text:
Abandoning invalidates JujutsuStateModel.repositoryStates, but this only refreshes the working copy of each repository. Abandoning anything other than the working copy means that there is no change, and nothing else gets refreshed.

Consequently, reloading the whole log will be inefficient with a large repository. We need a way to reload just the tree of the log that has changed, sugically replace that, and ensure that views of the log are updated accordingly.

author: Kevin Thomas
text: See JujutsuGraphAndDescriptionRenderer.GraphAndDescriptionPanel.drawLinesToParents - draw horizontal lines and arcs instead of diagonal lines

author: Kevin Thomas
text: This is using 0.3.3 of the plugin

author: Kevin Thomas
text: Linked with UI freezes only because this could be a threading issue. Note that the UI didn't lock up when this error occurred.

author: Kevin Thomas
text: This is becoming less important now that the custom log has most of the features of the standard log.

author: kevin
text: When loading a non-JJ project and setting the project VCS to JJ, the JJ (working copy and log) windows are not present. While this is correct, it would be helpful to show at least one of the windows with a message that there is no JJ repository in the specified root, and give the user the opportunity to init (or git init if git is present).

author: kevin
text: We need an init action that takes a root and a colocate flag. Consistent with Git init, the VCS menu should have a 'Initialise Jujutsu Repository...' action. This needs to request a directory (via a File/Open dialog). If the directory is already initialised (or a directory above contains .jj), warn the user before continuing. If the directory (or one above) already has a git repo and the user has opted not to colocate, warn. Investigate options for requesting both a directory and a colocate checkbox. Ideally, this would be a dialog with both on it. The file dialog may need to be a modal from there (so the initial dialog contains the directory name with a button to pick the directory).

author: Kevin Thomas
text: See usw to rl in the jj-idea repo, for example (14/01/2026)

author: Kevin Thomas
text: Investigate git4idea. It looks like it has a sensible UX whereby the commit details contains a union of the selected commits, i.e. the changes list shows all changes from all selected commits (presumably handling duplicates where files change in multiple selected commits) and the detail panel shows a list of details, i.e. all change id/commit id/branch/description etc. in a mini-panel per commit. This should be consistent with that, where possible. If merging changes is difficult, start with a simple implementation and break more complex edge cases out into separate tasks, so long as the edge cases degrade gracefully.

author: kevin
text: Ensure that significant changes update the change log

author: Kevin Thomas
text: Some actions support multi-select, but others (e.g. diff) silently accept only the first selected change. Update all actions from the commit details panel (i.e. on individual file changes within a commit) to either support multi-select or be disabled if they cannot.

author: kevin
text: Can describe only *mutable* changes. Therefore this should be dependent on jj-idea-8u4.

author: kevin
text: This is already possible. The new change action already requests a description, and it can be invoked from a specific point in the log. Is there any more to be done here?
